title: Project MMO: Farmer's Delight Compat
description: Farmer's Delight cooking blocks now grant XP for the items they create.
body:
This mod requires Farmer's Delight and Project MMO to use.

About

This mod adds compatibility between Project MMO and Farmer's Delight and likely most of the Farmer's Delight expansion mods if their recipes use the Farmer's Delight cooking blocks.

The Stove, Cooking Pot, and Skillet will now grant XP for the SMELT event and the Cutting Board will grant XP for the CRAFT event. 

 

How To Use

In order to get this to work, simply add some XP for the SMELT or CRAFT events on any of the relevant food items that these blocks can create and you're good to go.

There is a difference between these cooking blocks and normal furnaces / campfires / crafting tables; these blocks give XP based on the resulting food item, NOT from the ingredients to make the item. This is because many recipes require multiple ingredients and it was much simpler to base the XP granted from the food item created. This will mean you need to specify XP on both the raw AND cooked products to give XP. If you cook vanilla beef with a normal vanilla furnace the XP granted is based off of the raw beef, not the steak that it creates. With Farmer's Delight cooking blocks the XP granted is based on the XP configured on the steak item instead.

You could use this to encourage players to use Farmer's Delight blocks to cook foods instead of the vanilla furnace / campfires by giving more XP on the steak item. If you want them to be the same just set both beef and steak (cooked_beef) to the same values.

 

Notes

This should be compatible with all other mods. Please report any bugs or issues to GitHub. If you're not getting XP for an item, make sure you've configured it correctly in Project MMO first.

 

It does have mixins for Farmers Delight, so it's possible a future Farmer's Delight update could break some of the mixins. If that were to happen I would likely fix it quickly, but in the meantime just don't update to the latest Farmer's Delight version if you want to keep using this. Farmer's Delight doesn't typically update more than once or twice a release, so this is unlikely for 1.20.1, and it is unlikely even if it did these mixins would break.

 

Feel free to use this mod in your modpacks, just have a link back to this mod if possible. This cannot be used in for profit modpacks or posted on any other sites.

 

Huge thanks to the developer of Project MMO, Caltinor, for tons of help on this mod (and many other things). Also huge thanks to LlamaLad7, the wizard of Oz (of mixins) on the NeoForge discord.
